metadata.name: squawk
metadata.description: Squawk — walkie-talkie micro prediction markets on MagicBlock Ephemeral Rollups

instructions.name: claim_round
docs: Claim a resolved/voided position into the member ledger. Signerless and
permissionless: winnings can only ever go to the position's own member,
so anyone (client auto-claim, host close-out loop) may fire it.

instructions.name: close_channel
docs: Instruction 9 — host closes the channel ON THE ER: marks it Closed and
schedules commit + undelegation of the channel plus every delegated
account passed in remaining_accounts (members; rounds in Phase 3).
Base layer only ever sees status Closed after the commit lands, so
withdraw's Closed gate is inherently safe (Settling state not needed).

instructions.name: commit_rounds
docs: Commit + undelegate a batch of delegated accounts (ER) without any
state change — used to release Round PDAs after close_channel so the
settlement bundle stays small. Permissionless: committing program
state back to base is always safe.

instructions.name: create_channel
docs: Instruction 2 — host creates the channel + its vault (ATA owned by the
channel PDA; the vault is never delegated).

instructions.name: create_channel_permission
docs: Private-channel (PER) read gate for the Channel account: host + every
member's wallet and session key may read it on the TEE ER; everyone
else is blocked at ingress. Runs ON the ER after delegation; the
channel PDA signs and pays the ephemeral rent (pre-funded on base
before delegation). remaining_accounts = this channel's Member PDAs.

instructions.name: create_member_permission
docs: PER read gate for one Member account: only the host and that member
(wallet + session key) may read it — individual stakes and balances
stay hidden from other players.

instructions.name: create_round
docs: Pre-creates the next Round PDA (base layer, while Open — before
delegation). Rounds are created sequentially; the host script makes
round_count of them, then delegates each after go_live.

instructions.name: create_round_permission
docs: PER read gate for one Round account: HOST ONLY — the pools are
invisible to players while staking. This is the blind bet; players
follow the round through the channel's board-mirror fields and see
the pools only when resolve_round reveals them there.

instructions.name: delegate_channel
docs: Instruction 4b — delegate the Channel PDA to the ER. Host-only, Live only.
`validator` targets a specific ER validator (the TEE for private channels).

instructions.name: delegate_member
docs: Instruction 4c — delegate one Member PDA to the ER. Host-only, Live only.
(The channel account may itself already be delegated; only its data is
read here, which survives delegation.)

instructions.name: delegate_round
docs: Instruction 4d — delegate one Round PDA to the ER. Host-only, Live only.

instructions.name: extend_channel
docs: Host extends the channel end time. Runs on the base layer before
delegation and on the ER while delegated — which makes it the Phase 2
proof op: after delegation it must fail on base and succeed on the ER.

instructions.name: go_live
docs: Instruction 4a (docs/plan.md §5.2) — host flips the channel Live.
Delegation of the channel/member accounts follows as separate
instructions (delegate_channel / delegate_member), composed by the
client, since each delegation is its own CPI.

instructions.name: initialize_config
docs: Instruction 1 (docs/plan.md §5.2) — one-time global config.

instructions.name: join_channel
docs: Instruction 3 — user deposits USDC into the vault, gets a Member ledger,
and registers the session key allowed to stake/claim on their behalf.

instructions.name: lock_round
docs: Instruction 7 — lock the round at locks_at. Signerless and
permissionless so both the MagicBlock crank and any client can fire it.

instructions.name: open_round
docs: Instruction 5 — host activates a pre-created round (ER).

instructions.name: resolve_round
docs: Instruction 8 — host resolves the round (host is the referee for the
MVP, disclosed in the UI). Accepts Locked, or Staking past locks_at so
a missed crank can't wedge the round. If the winning side has no
stakes, the round voids and claims refund everyone.

instructions.name: schedule_lock_crank
docs: Schedules a one-shot MagicBlock crank (ER) that fires lock_round at
the round's locks_at. lock_round stays permissionless, so a client can
always lock as fallback if the crank misses (docs/plan.md §5.2 item 7).

instructions.name: stake
docs: Instruction 6 — stake from the member ledger into a round pool (ER).
Signed by the member's wallet OR their registered session key.

accounts.name: signer
docs: Member wallet OR registered session key (checked in the handler).

instructions.name: withdraw
docs: Instruction 10 — redeem the Member ledger balance as real USDC.
Allowed while Open (back out before go_live) or Closed (after
settlement has committed back); never while Live/Settling, when the
ledger is delegated and in play.

errors.name: TitleTooLong
errors.msg: Title exceeds 64 bytes

errors.name: QuestionTooLong
errors.msg: Question exceeds 128 bytes

errors.name: InvalidEndsAt
errors.msg: ends_at must be in the future

errors.name: InvalidAmount
errors.msg: Amount must be greater than zero

errors.name: ChannelNotOpen
errors.msg: Channel is not open for joining

errors.name: WithdrawLocked
errors.msg: Withdrawals are locked while the channel is live or settling

errors.name: NothingToWithdraw
errors.msg: Nothing to withdraw

errors.name: Overflow
errors.msg: Arithmetic overflow

errors.name: Unauthorized
errors.msg: Only the channel host may do this

errors.name: ChannelNotLive
errors.msg: Channel is not live

errors.name: SessionKeyInvalid
errors.msg: Signer is neither the member nor their session key

errors.name: RoundOutOfOrder
errors.msg: Rounds must be created in order

errors.name: RoundNotPending
errors.msg: Round is not pending

errors.name: RoundNotStaking
errors.msg: Round is not open for staking

errors.name: RoundLockPassed
errors.msg: Round is already past its lock time

errors.name: RoundNotLockable
errors.msg: Round lock time has not been reached

errors.name: RoundNotResolvable
errors.msg: Round is not resolvable yet

errors.name: RoundNotResolved
errors.msg: Round is not resolved

errors.name: InvalidRoundTiming
errors.msg: Invalid round timing

errors.name: InsufficientBalance
errors.msg: Insufficient ledger balance

errors.name: OppositeSide
errors.msg: Position is on the opposite side

errors.name: PositionPending
errors.msg: Previous position must be claimed first

errors.name: NothingToClaim
errors.msg: No open position to claim

errors.name: WrongRound
errors.msg: Position belongs to a different round

errors.name: InvalidVisibility
errors.msg: Visibility must be 0 (public) or 1 (private)

errors.name: ChannelNotPrivate
errors.msg: Channel is not private

errors.name: WrongChannel
errors.msg: Account does not belong to this channel

types.name: Channel
docs: The room — seeds `["channel", channel_id: u64 le]`. Delegated while Live.

fields.name: total_pool
docs: Sum of all member deposits currently held in the vault (ledger total).

fields.name: visibility
docs: 0 = public ER, 1 = private (TEE PER, blind betting + unlisted).

fields.name: active_question
docs: Board mirror: the active round's display data duplicated onto the
channel. On a private ER the Round accounts are host-only-readable,
so members follow play through these fields instead. Written
unconditionally (harmless on public channels).

fields.name: active_round_status
docs: RoundStatus of the active round, as u8.

fields.name: reveal_yes
docs: Resolve-time pool snapshots, revealed at resolve (zero while staking).

fields.name: last_outcome
docs: 0 = none, 1 = yes, 2 = no, 3 = voided.

types.name: Config
docs: Global config — seeds `["config"]`.

types.name: Member
docs: Per-user channel ledger — seeds `["member", channel_key, user]`.
Delegated while Live; only ledger numbers move on the ER, never tokens.

fields.name: deposited
docs: Lifetime deposits (history; not zeroed on withdraw).

fields.name: balance
docs: Withdrawable ledger balance; updated in the ER as rounds resolve.

fields.name: session_key
docs: Session key allowed to sign stake/claim_round for this member.

fields.name: position
docs: The single open position (amount == 0 → none).

types.name: Position
docs: The member's single open position, embedded in `Member` (deviation from
docs/plan.md §5.1 standalone PDAs — see docs/decisions.md Phase 3): rounds
are sequential, so one open position per member suffices and no accounts
ever need creating on the ER. `amount == 0` means no open position. A
second stake on the same side adds to `amount`; the opposite side is
rejected; staking a new round requires claiming the old position first
(clients auto-claim on resolution).

types.name: Round
docs: One micro-question — seeds `["round", channel_key, round_index: u16 le]`.
Delegated while Live.

fields.name: yes_pool
docs: Live pools: stakes flow in during Staking, claims deduct after resolve.
Whatever remains after all claims is unclaimed/dust and stays accounted.

fields.name: snap_yes
docs: Pool snapshots taken at resolve time — payout ratios use these so
claim order can't change anyone's share.
